underlying_tool: Stanford DRMcv.R-style OLS (log10 fold ~ binary mutation presence), 5-fold CV, Python/sklearn reimpl (shared machinery with the NNRTI baseline)
cutoff_note: UNIFORM illustrative fold>=3 (NOT per-drug clinical); both models scored at the same cutoff so the DELTA is the fair wrapper-vs-tool signal; absolute calibration needs per-drug clinical cutoffs (v0.1, not sourced here)
interpretation: small delta => the deterministic catalog MATCHES the full regression (adds interpretability, not error); large positive delta => per-drug/mutant signal the catalog misses (bounds a v0.1 refinement)
citation: Rhee 2003 Nucleic Acids Res 31:298-303; method Stanford DRMcv.R
